content.full_text:
COUNTY OF CALAVERAS

OFFICE OF COUNTY COUNSEL
GOVERNMENT CENTER
SAN ANDREAS, CALIFORNIA 95249
Telephone (209) 754-6314

Jeffrey Tuttle
County Counsel

Spencer B. Batchelder
Assistant County Counsel

Adrienne M. Finch
Deputy County Counsel

September 30, 1992

Ms. Jeanette Turvill
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, #800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Request for Opinion

Dear Ms. Turvill:

The spouse of a candidate for Calaveras County Supervisor is
currently employed by Operating Engineers Local Union #3 (OE3).
The Calaveras County Employees Association (CCEA) is affiliated
with OE3. As compensation for the services of OE3, approximately
180 members of the CCEA pay dues of $22.00 a month to OE3. The
spouse of the candidate is assigned by OE3 as the union representative of the CCEA.

The spouse receives a salary from OE3 in excess of $500.00 per
year. OE3 also provides his office space, clerical support,
travel and other expenses. His salary and benefits are set and
paid by OE3 and CCEA has no control over the amount of his salary
or benefits. He receives no compensation directly from CCEA. As
the CCEA union representative, he provides the following services:

1. Assists the CCEA in negotiating its contract with the Board
of Supervisors regarding wages, benefits and working conditions. This includes all negotiations pursuant to the
Meyers-Milias-Brown Act.

2. Advises the CCEA in matters of law and policy relative to
the Meyers-Milias-Brown Act, County Personnel Policy, and
other laws and regulations.

3. Represents members of the union in grievances with the
County.

4. Represents CCEA members in disciplinary hearings up to and
including arbitration.

5. Provides advice on CCEA member benefits.

6. Assists with health insurance claims problems.

Ms. Jeanette Turrill
September 30, 1992
Page 2

_____________________________

7.      Makes Worker's Compensation referrals.

8.      Advises the CCEA on political action.

These duties are carried out by the spouse with a high degree of
autonomy.  The CCEA contract expires in 1993.  If the candidate is
elected, she will take office in January, 1993.  We will immediately be faced by a number of issues regarding her participation in
making decisions regarding the CCEA.  If elected as Supervisor,
would the candidate be prohibited from making or participating in
making decisions regarding the following:

1.      Negotiations with the CCEA pursuant to the Meyers-Milias-
        Brown Act and decisions regarding the County's Memorandum
        of Understanding with CCEA.

2.      CCEA member grievances.

3.      Disciplinary hearings involving CCEA members.

4.      CCEA member worker's compensation claims.

5.      Any issue in which the spouse is advising the CCEA or its
        members.

6.      Would the candidate be allowed to participate in decisions
        regarding the CCEA or its members if the spouse is not
        involved in the specific matters under consideration?

7.      If the candidate's spouse ceased representation of the
        CCEA, would the candidate then be eligible to participate in
        making decisions regarding the CCEA and its members?  If
        the answer to this questions is yes, would the candidate
        have to wait a year prior to participating in such decisions?

I would appreciate it if you could provide me with a written
opinion answering these questions as soon as possible.  Please do
not hesitate to contact me should you have any questions.

Sincerely,

JEFFREY TUTTLE
COUNTY COUNSEL

JT/gg

Sincerely, JEFFREY TUTTLE COUNTY COUNSEL JT/gg
